body:
verification_command=ddx bead show axon-243091c3 | rg 'axon-77f3a5ca|axon-5956e527|axon-100687fd|decomposed' && ddx bead show axon-77f3a5ca && ddx bead show axon-5956e527 && ddx bead show axon-100687fd
exit_code=0
output:
Deps:     axon-51d884f0, axon-ad2a9669, axon-77f3a5ca, axon-5956e527, axon-100687fd
ID:       axon-77f3a5ca
Title:    build(feat-009): Cypher executor streaming runtime foundation
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   helix, feat-009, area:cypher, area:storage, kind:feature
Desc:     Implement the missing axon-cypher executor foundation required before a StorageAdapter backend can exist. Add the QueryStore trait, streaming execute(plan, store) runtime, and in-memory store/test fixtures described by the closed executor dependency so downstream backend implementations have a concrete contract to implement.
Accept:   AC1. crates/axon-cypher/src/executor.rs exposes execute(plan, store) over the planner ExecutionPlan. AC2. crates/axon-cypher/src/memory_store.rs implements QueryStore for in-memory entity/link fixtures. AC3. The DDx ready/blocked query runs parse -> validate -> plan -> execute against a 10-bead/15-link fixture and returns expected ready and blocked beads. AC4. At least 10 executor integration tests cover DDx ready/blocked, dependency DAG, reachability, DISTINCT, OPTIONAL MATCH, EXISTS true/false, count(*), ORDER BY ASC/DESC. AC5. Bounded-memory streaming and 30-second timeout are enforced. AC6. cargo test -p axon-cypher and cargo clippy -p axon-cypher --all-targets -- -D warnings pass.
Created:  2026-05-04 00:05:45
Updated:  2026-05-04 00:05:45
spec-id: FEAT-009
ID:       axon-5956e527
Title:    build(feat-009): Cypher QueryStore over StorageAdapter
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   helix, feat-009, area:cypher, area:storage, kind:feature
Deps:     axon-77f3a5ca
Desc:     Implement the production QueryStore implementation for axon-cypher using axon-storage::StorageAdapter primitives. The implementation must use portable get/range_scan/index_lookup/link primitives only and must not introduce SQLite- or PostgreSQL-specific query operators.
Accept:   AC1. axon-cypher has a StorageAdapter-backed QueryStore implementation available to callers. AC2. Entity label/property reads use StorageAdapter index_lookup or bounded range_scan according to the planner operator. AC3. Relationship expansion and EXISTS probes use StorageAdapter link primitives or the dedicated portable link API, not backend-specific SQL/JSON operators. AC4. Errors from storage are mapped into CypherError without unwrap() in library code. AC5. Unit tests cover scan, index lookup, expand, incoming/outgoing links, and EXISTS probes through a fake or memory StorageAdapter. AC6. cargo test -p axon-cypher and cargo clippy -p axon-cypher --all-targets -- -D warnings pass.
Created:  2026-05-04 00:05:54
Updated:  2026-05-04 00:06:14
spec-id: FEAT-009
ID:       axon-100687fd
Title:    test(feat-009): SQLite Cypher executor parity and ready/blocked benchmarks
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   helix, feat-009, area:cypher, area:storage, kind:feature
Deps:     axon-5956e527
Desc:     Add SQLite-backed integration coverage and DDx ready/blocked performance evidence for the StorageAdapter-backed Cypher executor. Reuse the executor integration scenarios against SqliteStorageAdapter and add the FEAT-009 ready/blocked latency gate datasets.
Accept:   AC1. The 10+ executor integration scenarios pass against SqliteStorageAdapter. AC2. DDx ready/blocked benchmark covers 1k beads and records p99 <100ms on dev hardware. AC3. DDx ready/blocked benchmark covers 10k beads and records p99 <500ms on dev hardware or documents a tracked follow-up if 10k exceeds local CI budget. AC4. Test/benchmark setup uses only StorageAdapter APIs and has no backend-specific query operators per ADR-010. AC5. cargo test --workspace passes. AC6. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes.
Created:  2026-05-04 00:06:01
Updated:  2026-05-04 00:06:14
spec-id: FEAT-009

verification_command passed: ddx bead show axon-243091c3 | rg 'axon-77f3a5ca|axon-5956e527|axon-100687fd|decomposed' && ddx bead show axon-77f3a5ca && ddx bead show axon-5956e527 && ddx bead show axon-100687fd
rationale: verification_command: ddx bead show axon-243091c3 | rg 'axon-77f3a5ca|axon-5956e527|axon-100687fd|decomposed' && ddx bead show axon-77f3a5ca && ddx bead show axon-5956e527 && ddx bead show axon-100687fd

Size-check result: decomposed, no implementation attempted.

Reason:
The bead was too large for one bounded execution pass after reading the current
worktree. The parent bead assumes the closed executor dependency provides
crates/axon-cypher/src/executor.rs and crates/axon-cypher/src/memory_store.rs,
but those files are absent in this worktree. The dependency commit recorded on
axon-ad2a9669 resolves to a checkpoint commit, not an executor implementation.

Implementing axon-243091c3 directly would therefore mix dependency repair,
streaming executor implementation, StorageAdapter production backend work,
SQLite integration parity, and performance benchmarks in one pass.

Child slices created:

- axon-77f3a5ca: build(feat-009): Cypher executor streaming runtime foundation
  Restores the missing executor foundation: QueryStore trait, execute(plan,
  store), in-memory store, and the 10+ executor scenarios from the dependency
  contract.

- axon-5956e527: build(feat-009): Cypher QueryStore over StorageAdapter
  Implements the production QueryStore over axon-storage::StorageAdapter using
  portable get/range_scan/index_lookup/link primitives only.

- axon-100687fd: test(feat-009): SQLite Cypher executor parity and ready/blocked benchmarks
  Reuses the executor scenarios against SqliteStorageAdapter and records the
  FEAT-009 DDx ready/blocked latency evidence at 1k and 10k beads.

Parent tracker update:
axon-243091c3 notes now record the decomposition and rationale. The parent
depends on all three children; axon-5956e527 depends on axon-77f3a5ca, and
axon-100687fd depends on axon-5956e527.
